title: Bidens fischeri
taxonomy: Bidens fischeri is classified as a species within the genus Bidens, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. The Bidens fischeri species is further classified as a member of the tribe Bidenteae, a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Bidens fischeri is also influenced by its phylogenetic relationships with other plants in the same genus. The scientific name Bidens fischeri is used to identify and distinguish this species from others within the genus.

intro: Bidens fischeri, also known as Bidens or Fischer's bidens, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens fischeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Bidens fischeri is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
